title: Xylosteon puniceum
intro: Xylosteon puniceum, also known as the purple osier or purple dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its showy white or pink flowers and purple berries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Xylosteon puniceum contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.

This species is native to parts of Europe, and it is also known as the scarlet cherry or the red waxy berry. The classification of Xylosteon puniceum reflects its unique characteristics and its position among other flowering plants.
